scenario:
---
name: review-intelligence
description: "E2E: Review Intelligence module — customer reviews and sentiment"
abortOnFailure: false
---

## Test
I'm PMM at Apify. What do customers say about Bright Data on review sites? I need sentiment analysis, common complaints, and what makes people switch. Work autonomously.

## Checkpoint

### Checks
contains: Bright Data
regex: https?://
regex: \[(?:High|Medium|Low)\s*[\|│]\s*[^\]]+\]
regex: (G2|g2|Capterra|capterra|Reddit|reddit|Glassdoor|glassdoor|Trustpilot|trustpilot)

### Script
INPUT=$(cat)
LINES=$(echo "$INPUT" | wc -l)
URLS=$(echo "$INPUT" | grep -oE 'https?://[^ )]+' | wc -l)
if [ "$LINES" -lt 15 ]; then
  echo "FAIL: report too short ($LINES lines)"
  exit 1
fi
if [ "$URLS" -lt 2 ]; then
  echo "FAIL: not enough source URLs ($URLS)"
  exit 1
fi
echo "PASS: $LINES lines, $URLS URLs"

### Judge
Evaluate this customer review intelligence report about Bright Data. It must:
1. Include real review data gathered via Apify actors (G2, Capterra, Reddit, or similar) — not just training knowledge
2. Provide sentiment analysis with specific data points (ratings, review counts, quotes)
3. Identify switching signals or common pain points
4. Include source URLs and confidence labels
5. Frame findings for a PMM audience with actionable takeaways
initBashScript:
set -e
WORKSPACE=$(find /tmp -type d -name 'eval-workspace-*' 2>/dev/null | head -1)
cd "$WORKSPACE"
git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/chocholous/apify-easy-competitive-intelligence.git _skill
cp -r _skill/.claude-plugin .claude-plugin
cp -r _skill/skills skills
rm -rf _skill
echo "Plugin injected"
